title: Excel vs. Anaplan vs. Custom Builds: What’s the real cost? ⚖️
body:
Did you outgrow Excel, but Anaplan’s $200K+ price tag doesn't add up? 📉 Consolidating data across 5+ entities is where most finance teams hit a wall. You're either stuck in manual spreadsheet hell or locked into a vendor contract for years.

We’ve built a vendor-neutral guide comparing the reality of Excel, Anaplan, and custom builds on Snowflake or BigQuery. No sales pitch—just hard data on costs and timelines.

Get the comparison here 👉 https://www.keboola.com/blog/you-outgrew-excel-now-what

We’re curious! Is the $200K+ price tag for EPM tools a dealbreaker for you too, or is the maintenance of a custom build the bigger "pain"? Let us know in the comments!

name: Ever been in a board meeting where three people are looking at three different numbers for the same 2026-02-13-73dbd62babe99382eb4e3734e9dbcea1
body:
Ever been in a board meeting where three people are looking at three different numbers for the same metric?

Not because anyone's wrong, but because no one formally owns what that metric actually means.

This is common #1 frustration we hear from CFOs.
It's not a technology problem. It's a definition problem.
The questions that break finance teams:

"How do we calculate EBITDA? Do we include depreciation from leased assets?"
"What counts as Marketing Cost? Just ad spend, or team salaries too?"
"When we say 'Customer,' is that one person globally, or per market?"

These aren't data questions. They're business questions.

Then you scale.

Once you need to manage 380 metrics across multiple countries, the real questions became:

→ Who approves when someone wants to change a definition?
→ How do you automate validation?
→ How do you get alerted when a KPI goes out of range?
→ How do you prevent the "which report is right?" debates?

Join us February 26 as Michal Hruška walks through a our framework for managing metrics at scale.

name: Ever been in a board meeting where three people are looking at three different numbers for the same 2026-02-13-ddbcfd151db9042768e8af4b0103f1a5
body:
Ever been in a board meeting where three people are looking at three different numbers for the same metric?

Not because anyone's wrong, but because no one formally owns what that metric actually means.

This is common #1 frustration we hear from CFOs.
It's not a technology problem. It's a definition problem.
The questions that break finance teams:

"How do we calculate EBITDA? Do we include depreciation from leased assets?"
"What counts as Marketing Cost? Just ad spend, or team salaries too?"
"When we say 'Customer,' is that one person globally, or per market?"

These aren't data questions. They're business questions.

Then you scale.

Once you need to manage 380 metrics across multiple countries, the real questions became:

→ Who approves when someone wants to change a definition?
→ How do you automate validation?
→ How do you get alerted when a KPI goes out of range?
→ How do you prevent the "which report is right?" debates?

Join us February 26 as Michal Hruška walks through a our framework for managing metrics at scale.

title: 380 Metrics, One Version of Truth: The Finance Leader's Playbook
body:
The Board asks: "What is our EBITDA?" Sales reports 12% growth, Finance says 9%, and Operations sees 8%.

Instead of making strategic decisions, you spend the next two weeks hunting for the "truth" in spreadsheets. This isn't a people problem—it’s a metric ownership problem.

🔹 Finance leaders must own their data definitions, not IT. 
🔹 If you don’t control the formula, how can you trust the decision? 
🔹 Learn how to standardize your financial language.

Still reconciling data on day 15? ⏳ Manual consolidation of multiple entities is a productivity killer that costs more than just time.

Leading finance teams are cutting their close from 15-25 days down to just 3-5. Free your team from "Excel hell" and shift your focus from data entry to strategic decision intelligence.

Download The Multi-Entity CFO's Survival Guide 👇
https://www.keboola.com/blog/the-multi-entity-cfos-financial-intelligence-guide

How many days does your monthly close currently take? Are you still stuck in Excel, or have you started moving toward automation? Let’s share some benchmarks in the comments! 💬

name: 380 Metrics, One Version of Truth: The Finance Leader's Playbook 2026-02-06-cbedadd13806d359dcae81aea7933159
title: 380 Metrics, One Version of Truth: The Finance Leader's Playbook
body:
The Board asks: "What is our EBITDA?" Sales reports 12% growth, Finance says 9%, and Operations sees 8%.

Instead of making strategic decisions, you spend the next two weeks hunting for the "truth" in spreadsheets. This isn't a people problem—it’s a metric ownership problem.

🔹 Finance leaders must own their data definitions, not IT. 
🔹 If you don’t control the formula, how can you trust the decision? 
🔹 Learn how to standardize your financial language.

📅 February 26, 2026 🕓 3pm GMT

👉 Register here https://www.keboola.com/events/why-your-board-sees-different-numbers-than-you-do

Everyone's talking about AI for finance. But here's the truth: AI won't help if your data definitions are a mess.

Build the foundation first: clear definitions and automated validation. The result? Board questions answered in minutes. Month-end close accelerated. And a solid foundation for AI.

🔹 Why finance should own definitions (because you understand the business)
🔹 Live Demo: See automated validation catching a Balance Sheet imbalance on day 1

The Board asks: "What is our EBITDA?" Sales reports 12% growth, Finance says 9%, and Operations sees 8%.

Instead of making strategic decisions, you spend the next two weeks hunting for the "truth" in spreadsheets. This isn't a people problem—it’s a metric ownership problem.

🔹 Finance leaders must own their data definitions, not IT. 
🔹 If you don’t control the formula, how can you trust the decision? 
🔹 Learn how to standardize your financial language.

📅 February 26, 2026 🕓 3pm GMT

👉 Register here https://www.keboola.com/events/why-your-board-sees-different-numbers-than-you-do

Instead of making strategic decisions, you spend the next two weeks hunting for the "truth" in spreadsheets. This isn't a people problem—it’s a metric ownership problem.

🔹 Finance leaders must own their data definitions, not IT. 
🔹 If you don’t control the formula, how can you trust the decision? 
🔹 Learn how to standardize your financial language.

name: Tariffs are changing. Are your margins safe? 2025-08-14-d3d06afb0c0671672820263b703866ee
title: Tariffs are changing. Are your margins safe?
body:
If you’re managing products impacted by tariffs, you already know the stress of not having clear answers.

In this webinar, we are showing you how to:

✅ Pull fresh tariff data from government sources via API
✅ Track landed cost and margin impact at the SKU level
✅ Simulate tariff scenarios before they hit your P&L

All using your own product, sales, and cost data - even if you are not full-time engineer.

name: Tariffs are changing. Are your margins safe? 2025-08-05-967115243aa5df9ec6e9dd6b8a8939bf
title: Tariffs are changing. Are your margins safe?
body:
If you’re managing products impacted by tariffs, you already know the stress of not having clear answers.

In this webinar, we are showing you how to:

✅ Pull fresh tariff data from government sources via API
✅ Track landed cost and margin impact at the SKU level
✅ Simulate tariff scenarios before they hit your P&L

All using your own product, sales, and cost data - even if you are not full-time engineer.

name: ‼️ Gamechanger in Data Pipeline development 2021-08-31-ec4d4ef762f34b45a6dcd5e38d265fe5
title: ‼️ Gamechanger in Data Pipeline development
body:
Here’s what you can do with Development branches
➡Shadow copy the storage data to a separated branch.
➡Describe your development branch and its purpose in markdown.
➡Do rebase to propagate the latest changes from production to the selected development branch to stay up-to-date.
➡Show all changes against production one-by-one in detail.
➡Merge selected or all changes to production.
➡The created development branch doesn't interfere with your production project in any way offering you a safe environment for any experiment.

name: Understand how Kebooola works and why users ❤️it 2021-08-27-ca4e90e13e791f959ca3ac57bc69fbb2
title: Understand how Kebooola works and why users ❤️it
body:
Description of layers from top to bottom:

🔷Connectors & Applications🔷
Modular “Lego” layer. All the data connectors, writers or applications that either provide you with their own logic or allow you to write your own logic, sit in this layer.

🔷Brain layer🔷
All the modern data stack issues are solved by this layer. Data observability, governance, and security are easily solvable. All meta data are stored here and are easily accessible. 

🔷Orchestration🔷
The kernel which allows us to connect infrastructure services with your workflows, code and external applications in one seamless workflow. This layer makes sure everything works together, is flexible, and auto scales. ETL/ELT, we do not care

🔷Infrastructure Independent🔷
Most valuable parts of the infrastructure that are needed for integration and analytics journey, wrapped with our APIs and made ready to be usable via just one click.

name: Join Our Free Webinar on eCom Competiton Monitoring 2021-04-18-b946ddabec4eb9eccd0d21d01d8f1573
title: Join Our Free Webinar on eCom Competiton Monitoring
body:
Do you know what is the biggest misconception about running a truly data-driven e-commerce business?

That it MUST be really expensive. At our webinar, we will show how you can benefit not only from your own data but from the information about your competitors as well, for very modest investments. We will showcase real-life examples from our partners (Apify, Revolt BI). 

🔵  Learn about various use cases for web scraping in e-commerce
🔵  See an end-to-end example of competition product monitoring
🔵  Learn how to build a similar process on your own quickly and easily
🔵  Meet people who can help

Sign up now, free of charge. See you on the other side! 🐙

name: Pushing Data into Salesforce: A Better Approach 2021-04-15-7cffebadececbd782f016565c62f77fe
title: Pushing Data into Salesforce: A Better Approach
body:
Getting data into Salesforce is for many an over-complex and daunting task, and the common solution is to rely on expensive tools or work with limitations like slow and limited uploads. 

However, there is a simpler way that helps you avoid the costs and saves you time by automating the process.

Download this free guide to setting up on-demand data sync in a few simple steps.

name: Work as a data scientist 2020-10-09-cde92d4fa99b4dcc2b9e9cde52eb370a
title: Work as a data scientist
body:
The all-in-one DataOps platform automates tricky tasks to free up your data science skills for more rewarding work:

✔️Centralizes all of your data into a single platform, so that you don’t have to waste time looking for it.
✔️Comes with a data catalog to help you understand the data and its lineage.
✔️Automates cleaning tasks, so that you can set-and-forget it.
✔️Native collaboration features, which allow you to work in parallel with your colleagues and share data.
✔️… and much more

Explore all the ways Keboola helps data scientists to be more creative, productive and independent: https://www.keboola.com/blog/data-scientist
